content: Just as 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems hierarchically compress episodic experience to prioritize high-uncertainty data for iterative improvement, general purpose autonomous agents can be redefined as nested memory hierarchies where each sub-agent acts as an active learning "memory entry" that votes on whether to query new environmental data based on its own local uncertainty. This structure eliminates the need for a centralized memory controller, letting the agent's overall memory capacity scale dynamically with task complexity.
